data.title: Sequoia Group Advances Advisory Capabilities with the First Collaborative Workspace Built for Smarter People Spend Decisions
data.description: Sequoia Group has enhanced advisor-client collaboration by introducing Sequoia Pages and unveiling the 2026 Total Compensation & Benefits Report. These initiatives aim to bolster data-driven advisory guidance. The company, based in San Francisco, made this announcement on March 27, 2026. Sequoia Group is known for being a leading provider of total compensation and benefits advisory services.

data.title: These Analysts Lower Their Forecasts On Oxford Industries Following Q4 Results
data.description: Oxford Industries Inc (NYSE: OXM) released its fourth-quarter results, showing a mixed performance. The company reported a loss of 9 cents per share for the quarter, falling short of the estimated earnings of 2 cents per share. However, quarterly sales reached $374.500 million, surpassing the expected $371.838 million. Additionally, Oxford Industries announced an increase in its quarterly dividend from 69 cents to 70 cents per share. Looking forward to FY2026, the company anticipates an adjusted EPS in the range of $2.10 to $2.70, below the market consensus of $2.78. Sales for the upcoming fiscal year are projected to fall between $1.475 billion and $1.530 billion, slightly below the estimated $1.500 billion. CEO Tom Chubb expressed optimism, highlighting stronger momentum in the Tommy Bahama business towards the end of the quarter.

data.title: VisionSys AI Inc. Announces Pricing of $3 Million Registered Direct Offering
data.description: On March 27, 2026, VisionSys AI Inc. disclosed it had initiated securities purchase agreements with particular institutional investors for acquiring 3,000,000 American Depositary Shares (ADS) or pre-funded warrants instead, priced at $1.00 per ADS. VisionSys is a technology services firm concentrating on brain-machine interaction ventures utilizing fundamental algorithms and associated software and hardware systems.

data.title: Why AI in Trading Execution Keeps Moving Toward Futures
data.description: In the automated execution realm, success is more dependent on market structure than advanced models. Recently in Hong Kong on March 27, 2026, the derivatives exchange OneBullEx made an announcement. This exchange, established in October 2025, strategically designed its platform architecture to prioritize futures execution from inception. By focusing on this core aspect, OneBullEx has achieved significant progress.

data.title: Dow Dives Into Correction As Chance Of Rate Hike Spikes: What Do Prediction Markets Think?
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones Industrial Average plummeted over 400 points, dipping into correction territory amidst concerns about wartime supply disruptions. Brent crude oil prices remained near $111 per barrel while the S&P 500 was on track for its fifth consecutive weekly decline, marking its longest losing streak since 2022. The rates market saw significant activity, with futures traders on Friday increasing the likelihood of a Fed rate hike by year-end to 52% according to the CME FedWatch tool, a significant surge from zero a month ago. Microsoft stock showed signs of weakness, leading to a decline in MSFT shares. Polymarket revealed a contract with a 25% chance of the Fed raising rates in 2026, accumulating $642,900 in volume. This contract had surged from 8% at the beginning of March. Similarly, Kalshi indicated a 27% chance, still lower than the 52% implied by CME futures. For more details, refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Commercial Metals Analysts Slash Their Forecasts After Q2 Results
data.description: Commercial Metals Co. (NYSE: CMC) recently released their fiscal second-quarter results, which showed adjusted earnings of $1.16 per diluted share, falling short of the estimated $1.30. However, sales were higher than expected at $2.132 billion compared to the $2.091 billion estimate. The company reported net earnings of $93.0 million, or 83 cents per diluted share, with net sales totaling $2.1 billion. This is a significant improvement from the previous year's figures of $25.5 million, or 22 cents per diluted share, on net sales of $1.8 billion. Peter Matt, the President and CEO of CMC, expressed satisfaction with the team's performance and highlighted a more than two-fold increase in core EBITDA compared to the previous year. Looking ahead, CMC anticipates a substantial increase in core EBITDA for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to the second quarter. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: UNCG Campaign Expands Scholarships for More Than 1,000 Students, Surpasses $266 Million
data.description: In Greensboro, North Carolina, as of March 27, 2026, over 1,000 present students at the University of North Carolina Greensboro (UNCG) have been awarded scholarships under the University's Light the Way initiative. This campaign successfully accumulated over $266 million to enhance accessibility and opportunities for students.

data.title: Peter Schiff Sounds Alarm On Bitcoin-Backed Mortgages
data.description: Economist Peter Schiff is expressing concern about a new housing finance initiative that allows borrowers to use digital assets for home purchases. Schiff believes this move benefits crypto holders at the expense of the public, describing it as a "free ride." The Crypto-Collateral Structure Fannie Mae is making its entry into the crypto-backed mortgage arena through a collaboration with Better Home & Finance Holding Co. and Coinbase Global Inc. Within this program, borrowers secure a conventional mortgage for the property while obtaining a separate second loan backed by Bitcoin or USDC. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Boris Becker's 1989 U.S. Open Champion Trophy Up For Auction, $350,000+ Expected
data.description: Prestige Memorabilia, a renowned leader in tennis memorabilia worldwide, has revealed the launch of a special auction featuring a highly significant tennis artifact. The item in the spotlight is Boris Becker's U.S. Open Singles Trophy, presented to him after winning the 1989 tournament. This prestigious piece of tennis history stands out as a remarkable collector's item.

data.title: TomoCredit Gains Real Adoption from TomoIQ, Powerful AI Native Financial Agent to Help Everyday Americans Navigate Money With Confidence
data.description: In San Francisco on March 27, 2026, TomoIQ introduced its enhanced AI agent tailored for everyday Americans seeking personal finance assistance. Backed by strong demand and thorough behavioral data, this AI native platform aims to provide guidance to users. The upgrade promises a more intelligent system to cater to the needs of individuals, showcasing the company's commitment to serving its customers. This innovative solution signifies a significant step forward in the realm of personal finance, indicating the platform's evolution in adapting to consumer requirements.

data.title: Crypto Insurance Sounds Great Until You Actually Need It—Here's The Ugly Truth
data.description: Coinbase, a well-known cryptocurrency exchange (NASDAQ: COIN), faced backlash after denying a claim of $100,000 worth of Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) theft by a Coinbase One subscriber. This subscriber, Matthew Allan, who is the Chief Risk Officer at Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ: INTU), had almost $100,000 in Bitcoin stolen from his Coinbase account. Despite subscribing to Coinbase One's $29.99-a-month service, which promises up to $1 million in account protection, Allan was informed that he was not eligible for reimbursement due to not activating specific security settings outlined in the terms and conditions. Coinbase emphasized that customers are accountable for all account activities, even in the event of a security breach caused by outside sources. The situation led to a legal battle where Allan sued Coinbase, prompting the court to mandate the complaint be resolved through private arbitration. The outcome of the case remains uncertain as Allan may not... (remaining details available on Benzinga.com)

data.title: LEE'S FAMOUS RECIPE CHICKEN EXPANDS IN SOUTH CAROLINA WITH SIX NEW LOCATIONS
data.description: Franchisee Harris Mullis, who owns and operates a Lee's Famous Recipe® Chicken outlet in Rock Hill, South Carolina, is driving growth for the brand. With intentions to establish six new locations in the state, Mullis is spearheading the expansion efforts. This development comes as part of the franchise's broader push for growth and market penetration. The award-winning franchisee's strategic approach is expected to significantly contribute to the increase in Lee's Famous Recipe® Chicken outlets and is likely to bolster the brand's presence in the region.
